selectionRules: Choose the highest-priority ready task whose dependencies are done.
Change one bounded concern per pass and preserve observable behavior unless the task says otherwise.
Run the narrowest relevant checks first, then the workspace validation commands listed by the task.
Update task status, notes, and source references in the same change as the implementation.
Regenerate agent memory after structural, import, export, route, manifest, or symbol changes.

tasks.title: Introduce deterministic agent memory indexes
acceptanceCriteria: Files, imports, manifests, public APIs, and symbols are indexed in Markdown.
The task queue is valid JSON with a checked schema and stable identifiers.
A stale-memory check is available through pnpm scripts and static validation.

tasks.title: Make game sections accessible and add persistent product media
acceptanceCriteria: Authenticated users without a character are redirected to a required character creation page before any game route renders.
Profile, inventory, and dashboard content remains reachable through visible, keyboard-operable navigation and disclosure controls.
Unavailable or empty contract, faction, and shop operation groups are omitted instead of displaying dead forms or placeholder collections.
Admins can upload, replace, and remove validated persistent product images, and public image delivery supports cache validation.
Market and shop product grids show compact images and summaries, with descriptions and supply/demand graphs in details.
Migration, route-contract, documentation, source-parse, unit, typecheck, build, and staging media proofs are recorded.
tasks.notes: Feature Pass 103 completed the source implementation; Feature Pass 105 subsequently replaced the temporary page-level disclosure router with dedicated routes and retained card-level native disclosures. Dependency-free migration, route-contract, documentation, and TypeScript parse checks run in this environment. Registry access is unavailable, so the pinned pnpm/dependency-backed typecheck, tests, build, live PostgreSQL image lifecycle, and browser accessibility proof remain part of VAL-001/VAL-002 rather than reopening the bounded feature task.

tasks.title: Promote dashboard, profile, and inventory sections to routed pages
acceptanceCriteria: The eight dashboard sections, six profile sections, and three inventory sections are individually routable pages.
The base dashboard, profile, and inventory URLs remain valid overview routes.
Cards within the routed section pages use accessible, independently controlled disclosure widgets.
Every new route is present in the categorized side navigation with one unambiguous active item.
Dashboard message and notification streams only connect on the pages that use them.
Route, navigation-unit, source-parse, static UX, documentation, and generated-memory validation pass.
tasks.notes: Feature Pass 105 replaced hash-addressed page sections with 17 routed pages, retained native disclosure behavior at the card level, reorganized the side navigation by gameplay domain, and scoped dashboard live streams to their owning routes. Dependency-free source parsing and static validation run in this environment; installed typecheck/build and browser proof remain under VAL-001.

tasks.title: Run the full installed-environment MVP proof
acceptanceCriteria: Dependencies install with the pinned pnpm and supported Node versions.
All migrations apply to a clean PostgreSQL database.
Static validation, typecheck, build, tests, runtime smoke, backup, and restore pass.
Every discovered failure is fixed or recorded as a new task with reproduction details.
tasks.notes: Feature Pass 100 generated pnpm-lock.yaml with pnpm 9.15.4 and passed dependency installation, the complete static gate, workspace typechecking, the production Next.js build, and all executable package tests. It also capped Next build parallelism through NEXT_BUILD_CPUS after the sandbox exposed excessive 55-worker page generation. VAL-001 remains open only for Docker/PostgreSQL migration, live app/worker smoke, backup, restore, Redis-required, and database integration proof. Feature Pass 101 upgraded the proof contract to use frozen-lockfile installation, include the production build, prove Redis-required overflow behavior, start the built Next.js server, keep the worker alive through a stability window, and persist artifacts/mvp-runtime-proof.json on success or failure. The infrastructure-backed execution remains open because this environment has no Docker, PostgreSQL client/server, Redis service, or installable pnpm binary.

tasks.title: Prove database-backed integration flows
acceptanceCriteria: Integration tests run against a disposable TEST_DATABASE_URL.
The test database is prepared and cleaned deterministically.
Failures include actionable route, query, and migration diagnostics.
tasks.notes: Feature Pass 102 expanded the integration suite to use production job/crime services and verify successful persistence plus rejected-action rollback. Live PostgreSQL execution remains required after VAL-001.

tasks.title: Decompose the character dashboard panel by bounded feature
acceptanceCriteria: CharacterPanel remains the orchestration boundary and no extracted module exceeds 500 lines without justification.
Data fetching, mutations, derived view models, and presentation sections are separated.
No API contract, route behavior, visible copy, or game rule changes.
Relevant web tests, typecheck, and build pass.

tasks.title: Split the database schema into domain modules
acceptanceCriteria: Schema tables and relations are grouped into domain modules with a stable barrel entrypoint.
Generated SQL identity, table names, columns, indexes, and relations remain unchanged.
Database package typecheck and migration validation pass.

tasks.title: Split admin database queries into focused services
acceptanceCriteria: Admin search, moderation, audit, enforcement, and rollback queries have separate modules.
The @drugdeal/db public API remains source-compatible or changes are migrated atomically.
Admin route-contract and RBAC validation pass.

tasks.title: Organize validators by API domain
acceptanceCriteria: Schemas are grouped into domain files with one stable public barrel.
Existing import names and validation behavior remain unchanged.
Validator tests and all route typechecks pass.

tasks.title: Replace placeholder package tests with executable baselines
acceptanceCriteria: Worker scheduling and retry behavior have deterministic unit tests.
Database pure helpers and query mapping logic have unit tests without requiring a live database where possible.
Shared UI components have render and contract tests.
No package test command is a placeholder echo.
tasks.notes: Feature Pass 100 executed the worker, database, UI, web, validator, game, and observability test suites through the pinned pnpm workspace as part of validate:ci.

tasks.title: Add production observability and abuse telemetry
acceptanceCriteria: Structured logs are shipped to an external sink with correlation identifiers.
Critical worker, API, auth, moderation, and economy anomalies create actionable alerts.
Sensitive values and message contents are redacted by default.
Operational runbooks document alert ownership and rollback steps.
tasks.notes: Feature Pass 100 added the vendor-neutral @drugdeal/observability package, recursive redaction, structured web/worker events, HTTP event and alert sinks, health configuration visibility, critical API/worker alerts, graceful worker lifecycle telemetry, tests, and an operations runbook. External sink deployment, alert ownership verification, dashboards, and live incident exercises remain.

tasks.title: Establish load, bot-resistance, and rate-limit proof
acceptanceCriteria: Representative read and mutation routes have documented load profiles and budgets.
Rate-limit behavior is tested with Redis required and fallback modes.
Automated abuse scenarios cover login, messaging, market, trade, gambling, and PvP surfaces.
Capacity and degradation findings are recorded with remediation tasks.

tasks.title: Complete jurisdictional legal and payment review
acceptanceCriteria: Privacy, terms, community rules, age requirements, and payment language receive qualified jurisdictional review.
Required consent, retention, deletion, refund, and support workflows are implemented.
Draft labels are removed only after approval evidence is recorded.
tasks.notes: Blocked on external legal review; implementation work may proceed only from reviewed requirements.
